mission.title: Install and Configure Kong Gateway
mission.description: Kong Gateway is a cloud-native API gateway that provides high performance and extensibility through plugins. It is ideal for managing microservices and API traffic, offering features like load balancing, authentication, and advanced AI traffic capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and management of plugins for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Kong Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Kong Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add kong https://charts.konghq.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Kong charts.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kong Gateway with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Kong Gateway in the 'kong' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kong

helm install kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

```

> Creates a namespace for Kong.
> Installs Kong Gateway version 3.9.1.

steps.title: Create a sample Custom Resource (CR)
steps.description:
This step creates a sample ingress resource to test the Kong Gateway functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: kong
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.kong.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

```

> Replace 'example-service' with your actual service name.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: example-ingress
  namespace: kong
spec:
  rules:
  - host: example.kong.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: example-service
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Kong Gateway installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Kong Gateway pods are running successfully.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> You should see the Kong Gateway pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kong Gateway
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kong Gateway installation and all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kong --namespace kong

kubectl delete namespace kong

```

> Uninstalls Kong Gateway from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kong Gateway
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations. This process includes draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kong -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kong kong/kong --namespace kong --version 3.9.1

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Drains the Kong pods.
> Upgrades Kong Gateway to version 3.9.1.
> Verify that the Kong pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kong -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong ingress controller not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kong ingress controller may not be installed or running.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Kong Gateway pods are running using 'kubectl get pods -n kong'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The backend service may not be reachable or not running.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the backend service using 'kubectl get svc' and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Ingress not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ingress resource may not be created or may have incorrect configurations.

**Fix:**
Verify the ingress resource with 'kubectl get ingress -n kong' and ensure it is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The database connection may be misconfigured or the database service may not be running.

**Fix:**
Check the database service status and configuration in the Kong values file.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new plugins and improved performance for existing plugins.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecation of legacy plugins that are no longer maintained.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.8, ensure to review the plugin configurations as some may require updates.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kong Gateway pods running with the output: '1/1 Running' for each pod when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kong'.

        backend:

            port:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
